prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a nearly black frame, with no discernible objects except a small watermark-like logo in the lower-right corner.

It then cuts to a dark, close-up product shot. A pair of fingers holds a small teal/green plastic container close to the camera. The label is readable: “Cool Mint LISTERINE POCKETPAKS” and “ORAL CARE STRIPS.” The container appears to be a small flip-top pack, shown against a dark, out-of-focus background. Only the hands and the product are visible.

Later, the scene changes to a clean product display on a white background. An orange/yellow container is centered on screen. The label reads “Fresh Citrus LISTERINE,” with smaller text below that appears to refer to PocketPaks/oral care strips. Beneath the product, large black text reads: “The Less Intense PocketPaks.™”

The same product shot continues, but the background shifts from plain white to a warmer yellow glow around the centered orange container, making the pack appear brighter and more highlighted. The bottom text remains: “The Less Intense PocketPaks.™”

The commercial then cuts or fades to black again, with the small lower-right watermark still visible. No additional visual information is shown in the final frame.
